notes: Sixteenth validated clue batch compiled from official school websites.
Clues intentionally avoid revealing enrollment size, grade span, or directional/proximity information because those are gameplay feedback channels.

clues.text: Official materials say the school has focused on quality education since 2007.
Its overview says four defining elements are protected learning environments, direct instruction, highest quality teaching staff, and high expectations.
The school says its community works as a team to provide a safe, clean, spirited, and morally strong environment.
Admissions pages call it the school with the highest academic growth in Columbus County.
Network governance pages list Colleen Combs as chair of the Board of Trustees.
Campus pages list Kimberly Patrick as headmaster.
Official materials say the school opened in August 2013 in the former Peabody Elementary building.
Its overview says four defining elements are protected learning environments, direct instruction, highest quality teaching staff, and high expectations.
The school says it replicates its sister schools in the Classical Charter Schools of America network.
Its academic program says students are prepared to become lifelong learners through proven curricula and strong foundations in critical thinking and communication.
Network governance pages list Colleen Combs as chair of the Board of Trustees.
A school announcement says Marcus Dos Santos was named headmaster in 2022.
Its public-facing tagline is "Empowering Students for the 21st Century."
Its mission says the school develops successful student leaders by growing self-efficacy, intellectual understanding, and social competence.
Official materials describe a Project Based Leadership model unique to the school.
The school says every student takes Digital Media courses involving graphic and game design, audio and video production, animation, and programming.
Its board page lists Roger Linville as board chairperson.
Official pages identify Justin T. Smith as managing director.
Its mission says the school pairs rigorous content with International Baccalaureate learner profile ideals.
Official materials say the school began operating in the 2016-17 school year.
The school says it is the only charter school in North Carolina and in the Charlotte area to offer International Baccalaureate to students beginning in sixth through twelfth grade.
Official pages say the school has been recognized multiple times by Charlotte Magazine Best of the Best and the Union County Macaroni Kid awards.
Its board page lists Jim Lovell as chair.
Official leadership pages list Joely Lord as executive director.
Official pages say the school is known as the home of the Flying Lions.
Its mission says the school develops in every student a foundation of knowledge, a practice of reason, a quality of eloquence, and a habit of virtue.
Official materials say the school started in the 2015-2016 school year and its first graduating class was the class of 2024.
School profile pages say the school earned Cognia accreditation in the summer of 2024.
Its board page lists Scott Sipe as president.
Official staff pages list Dr. Dana Coyle as executive director and grammar school dean.
